rulings.Case Title: CLEVELAND WRECKING COMPANY, A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. WEST BAY BUILDERS, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 5, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF CLEVELAND WRECKING COMPANY, A DELAWARE CORPORATION MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES The motion is granted. The Court concludes that apportionment is not appropriate in this case because the claims were inextricably intertwined. The Court, however, concludes that the claimed fees are excessive. Plaintiff can recover $373,271 as reasonable fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: CHICAGO TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. BURL STEVEN WALKER et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 5, 2012, Line 2, CROSS DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK N.A. AS TRUSTEE FOR OPTION ONE, HOMEWARD RESIDENTIAL, INC., SAND CANYON CORP. F/K/A OPTION ONE MORTGAGE CORP. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT Overruled. Cross-Complainant has pled facts sufficient to allege the sixth and seventh causes of action. Whether the one month postponement was reasonable is a question of fact not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. The pleadings are not uncertain. rulings.Case Title: ATLANTIC CREDIT AND FINANCE INC. VS. MICHAEL G. ANGELES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions; Dec; Memo And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 5, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF ATLANTIC CREDIT AND FINANCE INC.'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: IT APPEARS THAT THERE IS NO OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION FILED BY ATLANTIC CREDIT AND FINANCE INC.; ACCORDINGLY, THE MOTION IS GRANTED. COUNSEL SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER FOR SUBMISSION TO DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND PRESENT THE PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON NOV. 5TH. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: TROY ROBERTS et al VS. FAMILY MOSAIC PROJECT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 5, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to plaintiff Redd for the race discrimination claim. Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to plaintiff Roberts for the disability discrimination and failure to accommodate claims. Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied as to plaintiff Roberts for the race discrimination claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WHITE OAK HOLDINGS, LLC et al VS. PHILIP N. DUFF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 5, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, CROSS COMPLAINANTS PHILIP DUFF, PATRICK DOWLING'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: CROSS-COMPLAINANTS' DUFF AND DOWLING'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO DOCUMENT REQUESTS NO.1 & 3 IS GRANTED. TO THE EXTENT THAT CROSS-DEFENDANTS CLAIM THAT IT HAS PREVIOUSLY PRODUCED ALL SUCH DOCUMENTS IT SHALL PRODUCE FOR CROSS-COMPLAINANTS INSPECTION A COPY OF THE SEARCH PROTOCOL TO ESTABLISH THAT THE SEARCH CRITERIA WAS BROAD ENOUGH TO IDENTIFY ALL DOCUMENTS REQUESTED BY REQUESTS NO.1 & 3. COUNSEL FOR MOVING PARTY WILL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER AND SUBMIT TO CROSS-DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND SUBMIT TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON NOV. 5, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT
